response:
{
  "children": [
    {
      "name": "CreateOrderHandler",
      "purpose": "Handle create order command: validate user, check stock, compute total, deduct stock, create pending order",
      "inputs": [{"name": "order_data", "type": "dict", "description": "Dictionary containing user_id and items list", "source": "parent"}],
      "outputs": [{"name": "result", "type": "dict", "description": "Result with success, message, and order_id in data", "consumer": "parent"}],
      "boundary": {"in_scope": ["user existence", "stock availability", "price calculation", "stock deduction", "order creation"], "out_of_scope": ["payment", "shipping", "cancellation"]},
      "behavior": "Extract user_id and items from order_data. Check user exists using users.get (via users.exists). For each item, get product, verify stock >= quantity. Compute total price = sum(price * quantity). Deduct stock from each product using products.update. Create order with user_id, items, total_price, status='pending' using orders.create. Return success dict with order_id.",
      "signature": "def CreateOrderHandler(order_data: dict) -> dict",
      "stop_decompose": true,
      "stop_reason": "Leaf node - handles a single command",
      "global_vars": [
        {"variable": "users", "op": "read", "description": "Check user existence"},
        {"variable": "products", "op": "read_write", "description": "Check stock and deduct quantities"},
        {"variable": "orders", "op": "write", "description": "Create new order record"}
      ]
    },
    {
      "name": "PayOrderHandler",
      "purpose": "Handle pay order command: validate order pending, check balance, deduct balance, update order to paid",
      "inputs": [{"name": "order_data", "type": "dict", "description": "Dictionary containing order_id", "source": "parent"}],
      "outputs": [{"name": "result", "type": "dict", "description": "Result with success, message, and order_id in data", "consumer": "parent"}],
      "boundary": {"in_scope": ["order existence", "order status pending", "balance sufficiency", "balance deduction", "order status update"], "out_of_scope": ["stock management", "shipping", "cancellation"]},
      "behavior": "Extract order_id from order_data. Get order via orders.get, verify status is 'pending'. Get user by order's user_id via users.get, verify balance >= order's total_price. Deduct total_price from user's balance via users.update. Update order status to 'paid' via orders.update. Return success dict.",
      "signature": "def PayOrderHandler(order_data: dict) -> dict",
      "stop_decompose": true,
      "stop_reason": "Leaf node - handles a single command",
      "global_vars": [
        {"variable": "users", "op": "read_write", "description": "Check and deduct balance"},
        {"variable": "orders", "op": "read_write", "description": "Check and update order status"}
      ]
    },
    {
      "name": "ShipOrderHandler",
      "purpose": "Handle ship order command: validate order paid, update status to shipped",
      "inputs": [{"name": "order_data", "type": "dict", "description": "Dictionary containing order_id", "source": "parent"}],
      "outputs": [{"name": "result", "type": "dict", "description": "Result with success, message, and order_id in data", "consumer": "parent"}],
      "boundary": {"in_scope": ["order existence", "order status paid", "order status update"], "out_of_scope": ["payment", "stock", "completion"]},
      "behavior": "Extract order_id from order_data. Get order via orders.get, verify status is 'paid'. Update order status to 'shipped' via orders.update. Return success dict.",
      "signature": "def ShipOrderHandler(order_data: dict) -> dict",
      "stop_decompose": true,
      "stop_reason": "Leaf node - handles a single command",
      "global_vars": [
        {"variable": "orders", "op": "read_write", "description": "Check and update order status"}
      ]
    },
    {
      "name": "CompleteOrderHandler",
      "purpose": "Handle complete order command: validate order shipped, update status to completed",
      "inputs": [{"name": "order_data", "type": "dict", "description": "Dictionary containing order_id", "source": "parent"}],
      "outputs": [{"name": "result", "type": "dict", "description": "Result with success, message, and order_id in data", "consumer": "parent"}],
      "boundary": {"in_scope": ["order existence", "order status shipped", "order status update"], "out_of_scope": ["payment", "stock", "shipping"]},
      "behavior": "Extract order_id from order_data. Get order via orders.get, verify status is 'shipped'. Update order status to 'completed' via orders.update. Return success dict.",
      "signature": "def CompleteOrderHandler(order_data: dict) -> dict",
      "stop_decompose": true,
      "stop_reason": "Leaf node - handles a single command",
      "global_vars": [
        {"variable": "orders", "op": "read_write", "description": "Check and update order status"}
      ]
    },
    {
      "name": "CancelOrderHandler",
      "purpose": "Handle cancel order command: validate order pending or paid, restore stock, refund if paid, update status to cancelled",
      "inputs": [{"name": "order_data", "type": "dict", "description": "Dictionary containing order_id", "source": "parent"}],
      "outputs": [{"name": "result", "type": "dict", "description": "Result with success, message, and order_id in data", "consumer": "parent"}],
      "boundary": {"in_scope": ["order existence", "order status pending or paid", "stock restoration", "balance refund", "order status update"], "out_of_scope": ["creation", "shipment", "completion"]},
      "behavior": "Extract order_id from order_data. Get order via orders.get, verify status is 'pending' or 'paid'. If status is 'paid', get user via users.get, refund total_price to user's balance via users.update. For each item in order, restore stock via products.update (increment stock). Update order status to 'cancelled' via orders.update. Return success dict.",
      "signature": "def CancelOrderHandler(order_data: dict) -> dict",
      "stop_decompose": true,
      "stop_reason": "Leaf node - handles a single command",
      "global_vars": [
        {"variable": "orders", "op": "read_write", "description": "Check and update order status"},
        {"variable": "products", "op": "read_write", "description": "Restore stock for each item"},
        {"variable": "users", "op": "read_write", "description": "Refund balance if order was paid"}
      ]
    },
    {
      "name": "ListOrdersHandler",
      "purpose": "Handle list orders command: list all orders with optional user_id and status filters",
      "inputs": [{"name": "order_data", "type": "dict", "description": "Dictionary with optional user_id and status filters", "source": "parent"}],
      "outputs": [{"name": "result", "type": "dict", "description": "Result with success, message, and data (list of orders)", "consumer": "parent"}],
      "boundary": {"in_scope": ["listing orders with filters"], "out_of_scope": ["order creation", "modification"]},
      "behavior": "Extract optional user_id and status from order_data. Call orders.list with those parameters. Return success dict containing the list of orders in data.",
      "signature": "def ListOrdersHandler(order_data: dict) -> dict",
      "stop_decompose": true,
      "stop_reason": "Leaf node - handles a single command",
      "global_vars": [
        {"variable": "orders", "op": "read", "description": "List orders with optional filters"}
      ]
    },
    {
      "name": "GetUserOrdersHandler",
      "purpose": "Handle get user orders command: get all orders for a user, compute total spent and status counts",
      "inputs": [{"name": "order_data", "type": "dict", "description": "Dictionary containing user_id", "source": "parent"}],
      "outputs": [{"name": "result", "type": "dict", "description": "Result with success, message, and data (orders list, total_spent, status_counts)", "consumer": "parent"}],
      "boundary": {"in_scope": ["retrieving orders by user", "computing aggregates"], "out_of_scope": ["order modification", "user creation"]},
      "behavior": "Extract user_id from order_data. Optionally check user existence via users.exists. Call orders.list(user_id=user_id) to get all orders for that user. Compute total_spent as sum of total_price of all orders. Compute status_counts as dict counting orders by status. Return success dict with these in data.",
      "signature": "def GetUserOrdersHandler(order_data: dict) -> dict",
      "stop_decompose": true,
      "stop_reason": "Leaf node - handles a single command",
      "global_vars": [
        {"variable": "orders", "op": "read", "description": "List orders for user"},
        {"variable": "users", "op": "read", "description": "Check user existence"}
      ]
    },
    {
      "name": "ListProductsHandler",
      "purpose": "Handle list products command: list all products with optional low stock threshold filter",
      "inputs": [{"name": "order_data", "type": "dict", "description": "Dictionary with optional low_stock_threshold", "source": "parent"}],
      "outputs": [{"name": "result", "type": "dict", "description": "Result with success, message, and data (list of products)", "consumer": "parent"}],
      "boundary": {"in_scope": ["listing products with optional filter"], "out_of_scope": ["product modification", "order management"]},
      "behavior": "Extract optional low_stock_threshold from order_data. Call products.list(low_stock_threshold) with that parameter. Return success dict containing the list of products in data.",
      "signature": "def ListProductsHandler(order_data: dict) -> dict",
      "stop_decompose": true,
      "stop_reason": "Leaf node - handles a single command",
      "global_vars": [
        {"variable": "products", "op": "read", "description": "List products with optional low stock filter"}
      ]
    }
  ],
  "decomposition_rationale": "The Order Management System is decomposed into eight handler functions, one per supported command (Create, Pay, Ship, Complete, Cancel, ListOrders, GetUserOrders, ListProducts). Each handler independently processes its command using the provided data interfaces, ensuring separation of concerns and no cross-calls between siblings. The parent function (not shown) acts as a router, parsing the 'command' input and invoking the appropriate child handler with the 'order_data'. This structure meets all functional requirements while maintaining data consistency through atomic operations within each handler."
}
